methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $3M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Vuecason is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Dival Banerjee that has raised $3M+ to date. The robotics and manufacturing sector comprises 1 public comparable (277810.KQ) and 8 private peers including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity. However, the peer universe lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with similar valuation tiers to support a reliable peer-median approach—only 7 same-sector companies exist, with none meeting the narrow filtering criteria.

The valuation analysis relies on the Stage-Progression method, which applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement. Using the $3M raised as a proxy base, this method yields an implied valuation of $8M. This approach assumes the company's capital raised correlates reasonably with post-money valuation at seed close and that the 2.5x multiplier reflects normalized market expectations for progression to Series A.

The triangulated range supported by available data is $6M (low) to $9M (high), with a median of $8M. The analysis is constrained by reliance on a single method; triangulation strength would materially improve with access to same-stage or adjacent-stage peer financials in the robotics sector. Key uncertainties include post-money seed valuation at close, unit economics, and whether peer comps in this nascent sector appropriately reflect Vuecason's specific technology and market positioning.
